extraction.one_line_summary: MCNULTY Brandon won from a three-man break after HEALY Ben attacked 35km out but got caught on the descent
tactical_patterns: Healy repeated 50km solo attack pattern from earlier stage win but proved replicable formula failed second time with pursuit.
UAE positioned McNulty in break despite holding Almeida on GC, revealing dual-track strategy prioritizing stage wins over conservative defense.
KOM battle between Healy and Rubio throughout climbs telegraphed who had best form before final decisive selection at 35km.

rider_signals.HEALY Ben: positive: strongest on climbs but caught on descent after 35km solo move, showing climbing superiority but time trial weakness
rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: positive: first Grand Tour stage win after five attempts demonstrates maturation, held Healy's wheel intelligently rather than chasing alone
rider_signals.ALMIRAIL Bruno: negative: lost 30+ seconds in GC group despite only defending 1:08 lead, suggesting vulnerability before mountain stages ahead
rider_signals.FRIGO Marco: positive: rejoined leaders after being dropped, then contested sprint showing resilience beyond initial climbing limit
